question: What's the best time to visit Fraser Coast QLD with kids?
answer: You can't go wrong visiting Fraser Coast year-round, but April to October is absolutely ripper! The weather's gorgeous – warm days without the summer humidity. Plus, you might spot humpback whales migrating (July-November). School holidays get busy, so if you can swing it, visiting during term time means quieter beaches and better prices at holiday parks. Just remember to slip, slop, slap whenever you visit!

question: Are there pet-friendly holiday parks in Fraser Coast QLD?
answer: Good news for fur parents! Several Fraser Coast holiday parks welcome your four-legged mates. Many parks have designated pet-friendly cabins or powered sites where dogs can stay. You'll usually need to keep them on-lead and clean up after them. Always ring ahead to confirm their pet policy and any additional fees. Some gorgeous dog-friendly beaches nearby too, so your pup can have a proper holiday!

question: How much do holiday parks cost in Fraser Coast QLD during school holidays?
answer: School holiday prices vary heaps depending on accommodation type. Powered sites typically run $45-80 per night, while cabins range from $150-350+ per night. Easter and Christmas school hols are priciest. Book early – like 6-12 months ahead – for better rates and availability. Off-peak visits can save you 30-40%. Many parks offer stay-pay deals too, so always check their websites directly!

question: What should I pack for a holiday park stay with young kids?
answer: Essentials: swimmers, sun protection, insect repellent, and thongs for the shower block! Pack bedding if staying in unpowered sites, though many cabins include linen. Bring outdoor toys, bikes, and beach gear. Don't forget a first-aid kit, familiar snacks for fussy eaters, and favourite bedtime books. Most parks have BBQs and playgrounds, so pack your cricket set and let the kids run wild!

question: What is there to do near Fraser Coast QLD holiday parks for families?
answer: You're spoilt for choice! Visit the stunning beaches at Hervey Bay, take a ferry to Fraser Island for adventure, or spot turtles at Mon Repos (November-March). The Esplanade has brilliant playgrounds and calm swimming. Check out Reefworld Aquarium, WetSide Water Park, or take a whale watching tour. Most holiday parks have pools and jumping pillows to keep little ones entertained on-site too!
